---
title: Redis Adapter
description: Production cache backed by ioredis.
icon: Server
---

## When to use

Use the Redis adapter for production deployments where you need persistent, shared cache state across multiple app instances.

## Installation

```bash
bun add @justwant/cache ioredis
```

## Setup

```ts title="lib/cache.ts"
import { createCache } from "@justwant/cache";
import { createRedisAdapter } from "@justwant/cache/adapters/redis";
import Redis from "ioredis";

const redis = new Redis(process.env.REDIS_URL!);

export const cache = createCache({
  adapter: createRedisAdapter({ client: redis }),
});
```

## Options

```ts
createRedisAdapter({
  client: Redis;          // ioredis client instance (required)
  prefix?: string;        // Key prefix applied to all entries
  serializer?: CacheSerializer; // Custom serializer (default: JSON)
})
```

| Option | Type | Description |
|--------|------|-------------|
| `client` | `Redis` | ioredis client instance |
| `prefix` | `string` | Prefix applied to every Redis key |
| `serializer` | `CacheSerializer` | Custom serialize/deserialize logic |

## Environment variable

```bash
# .env
REDIS_URL=redis://localhost:6379
```

## Cluster support

Pass an ioredis `Cluster` instance to use Redis Cluster:

```ts
import { Cluster } from "ioredis";

const cluster = new Cluster([{ host: "127.0.0.1", port: 6379 }]);
const adapter = createRedisAdapter({ client: cluster });
```
